name: Argentona-Castell de Burriac Trail
name_original: De l'Argentona al Castell de Burriac
description: Hike the moderate 4.8-mile Argentona-Castell de Burriac Trail for panoramic views and a visit to the historic Burriac Castle.
region_intro: Hiking around Argentona offers access to diverse landscapes within the Maresme region of Catalonia, Spain. The area is characterized by its proximity to the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, providing a rich environment of wooded areas and varied terrain. Trails frequently lead to historical points of interest such as Burriac Castle, which offers panoramic views. Natural springs are also a common feature along many routes, contributing to the region's natural appeal for hikers. Best hiking trails around Argentona The most popular hiking route is Argentona-Castell de Burriac Trail, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This route leads to the historic Burriac Castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Esquirol Spring – Font del Ferro de Cabrera loop from Argentona, a moderate 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path. This trail explores natural springs within the local landscape. Local hikers also love the Font of the Camí d'Argentona – Burriac Castle loop from Argentona, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain to Burriac Castle, often completed in about 3 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Argentona is defined by the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, historical sites like Burriac Castle, and natural springs. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging treks. The routes in Argentona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 1,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Argentona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Argentona?
A: Argentona offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy paths to more challenging treks, primarily within the scenic Parc de la Serralada Litoral.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Argentona?
A: Hikers in Argentona can expect varied terrain, including paths that lead through lush wooded areas and trails that offer glimpses of the coastline from higher elevations. The routes often provide opportunities to explore natural monuments and mountain peaks within the Parc de la Serralada Litoral.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Argentona?
A: Yes, Argentona has several easy paths suitable for all skill levels, including beginners and families. While specific 'easy' routes aren't detailed here, the overall network includes options that are less strenuous, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the area.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Argentona?
A: Many of the trails around Argentona are designed as loops, offering convenient circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5333538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font of the Camí d'Argentona – Burriac Castle loop from Argentona&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail that takes you through varied terrain to the historic Burriac Castle.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see while hiking in Argentona?
A: A prominent landmark is the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1319371052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burriac Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views of the surrounding area and is a frequent destination on many trails. You can also discover natural springs, such as the Esquirol Spring and Font del Ferro de Cabrera, which are featured on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31557749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esquirol Spring – Font del Ferro de Cabrera loop from Argentona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Argentona?
A: The hiking trails in Argentona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the historical points of interest like Burriac Castle, and the well-maintained paths that offer options for various ability levels.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Argentona?
A: The Parc de la Serralada Litoral, which encompasses many of Argentona's trails, generally welcomes dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Argentona?
A: Argentona offers pleasant hiking conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are advisable, while winter offers cooler, crisp air for exploration.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Argentona?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate, Argentona does offer more challenging treks for experienced hikers. These routes typically involve greater elevation gain and longer distances, providing a good workout and rewarding views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19183287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burriac Castle – Esquirol Spring loop from Argentona&lt;/a&gt; covers over 11 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Where can I find mountain passes or huts near Argentona?
A: You can explore several mountain passes in the vicinity of Argentona, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/913262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parpers Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/821329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Òrrius Pass&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in huts, options like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5803300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cabanya dels Caçadors&lt;/a&gt; are also available in the surrounding area.

Q: What is the typical duration and elevation gain for hikes in Argentona?
A: Hikes in Argentona vary significantly. You can find shorter walks under two hours with less than 300 meters (1,000 feet) of elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31557749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esquirol Spring – Font del Ferro de Cabrera loop&lt;/a&gt;. Longer expeditions can exceed five hours and involve over 900 meters (3,000 feet) of elevation, catering to more ambitious hikers.
tips: Casa Puig i Cadafalch is a modernist house in Argentona. Built between 1897 and 1905, it was the summer home of the well-known architect and president of Catalonia Josep Puig i Cadafalch.
Can Mercader is another modernist villa in Argentona. With a height of 93 meters at its highest part, it is made up of 3 units.

The remains of Burriac Castle are almost a thousand years old. They are located at the top of Burriac Hill. One of the best ways to get to the castle is by following the local trail SL-C 115 from Cabrera de Mar.
The Cross of the Bei is in the Park of the Coastal Mountain range, concretely in Cabrils (the Maresme).
Fountain going up to the Castle of Burriac by the route of the sources of Argentona

faqs.question: What is the difficulty and terrain like for the Argentona-Castell de Burriac Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered moderate, requiring good physical fitness due to a constant ascent with significant elevation gain. The terrain varies, including paths through pine and holm oak forests, rocky sections like the "Roques Encantades", and potentially soft or slippery dry riverbeds after rain. Some parts may involve tracks or paved roads closer to the summit.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historic Burriac Castle, offering superb 360-degree panoramic views of the sea, mountains, and towns. Other notable features include the distinctive rock formations of the "Roques Encantades", the Casa Puig i Cadafalch, Can Mercader (Argentona), Avellà Cross, and the Esquirol Spring.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail often starts near the Font Picant in Argentona. While specific parking details are not provided, you would typically look for public parking within Argentona, close to the starting point of the hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it is located within the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, dogs should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. For particularly unique and breathtaking views, it is highly recommended to visit at sunrise or sunset. Be mindful of warmer temperatures in summer and potential slippery conditions in dry riverbeds after rain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Parc de la Serralada Litoral?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike within the Parc de la Serralada Litoral. However, visitors are expected to respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the Eix Central del Maresme, Argentona-Castell de Burriac, Cabrera de Mar-Castell de Burriac, and El Camí IT7TR2 El Camí de Llevant: de Montserrat a Salses.

faqs.question: What is a good insider tip for hiking to Castell de Burriac from Argentona?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for a constant ascent with few flat sections when starting from Argentona. This route is more challenging than those from other nearby towns. To make the most of the panoramic views, consider timing your hike to reach the castle at sunrise or sunset.

faqs.question: What kind of natural scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience varied Mediterranean landscapes, including forests of holm oaks and pines, areas with distinctive rock formations like the "Roques Encantades", and various Mediterranean shrubs. As you ascend, you'll gain splendid views of the pre-coastal area, the Montseny mountain range, and eventually the sea and Maresme coast.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with young children or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, constant ascent, and occasional rocky or uneven paths, this specific route from Argentona is not ideal for young children or absolute beginners. It requires good physical fitness and sure-footedness. There might be easier routes to the castle from other starting points.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months, and wear appropriate hiking footwear with good grip for varied terrain. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is advisable, and layers of clothing are recommended as temperatures can change with elevation.
